01-ai-sales-revolution.instruction: List 5 weekly sales tasks that are repetitive. Rate each on automation potential (high/medium/low) and explain why.
01-ai-sales-revolution.hint: 1. Updating CRM after calls — High — structured data entry, AI can parse call notes. 2. Sending follow-up emails — High — templatable with personalization. 3. Researching new prospects — High — web scraping + summarization. 4. Creating weekly pipeline reports — Medium — needs human judgment on deal quality. 5. Negotiating contracts — Low — requires relationship context and real-time adaptation.
01-ai-sales-revolution.goodExample: 1. CRM updates after calls — High — AI can parse call transcripts and auto-fill fields. 2. Follow-up emails — High — templatable, AI personalizes per prospect. 3. Prospect research — High — AI scrapes LinkedIn, news, financials in seconds. 4. Weekly pipeline reports — Medium — AI drafts but human validates deal stages. 5. Contract negotiation — Low — requires live rapport and judgment.
01-ai-sales-revolution.successMessage: Your sales automation hit list is ready. Start with the high-potential tasks — that's where AI gives you hours back.

01-ai-sales-revolution.instruction: Write a prompt asking AI to calculate how many hours per week a sales team could save by automating their top 3 time-consuming tasks.
01-ai-sales-revolution.placeholder: Calculate time savings for a sales team that...
01-ai-sales-revolution.hint: Our 5-person sales team spends: 8 hrs/week on CRM data entry, 6 hrs/week writing follow-up emails, 5 hrs/week researching prospects. Calculate: hours saved per week if AI handles 80% of each task, annual cost savings at $50/hr, and what reps could do with recovered time.
01-ai-sales-revolution.badExample: How much time can AI save my sales team?
01-ai-sales-revolution.goodExample: My 5-person sales team spends weekly: CRM entry (8 hrs), follow-up emails (6 hrs), prospect research (5 hrs). If AI handles 80% of each: calculate hours saved per person and team-wide, annual dollar savings at $50/hr loaded cost, and suggest how to reinvest recovered time into revenue-generating activities.
01-ai-sales-revolution.successMessage: ROI in black and white. This is how you get leadership to invest in AI tools — show the math.

02-lead-research-and-qualification.instruction: Write a prompt to research and qualify a specific company as a sales lead. Include what info to gather, qualification criteria, and desired output format.
02-lead-research-and-qualification.hint: Research Acme Corp as a potential lead for our B2B SaaS project management tool ($500/mo). Gather: company size, industry, tech stack, recent news, key decision makers. Qualify against: 50+ employees, uses PM tools, growing, in tech/consulting verticals. Output: qualification score (1-10), summary, recommended next step, and draft connection request.
02-lead-research-and-qualification.goodExample: Research Acme Corp for our $500/mo PM SaaS. Gather: size, industry, tech stack, news, decision makers with LinkedIn. Qualify: 50+ employees, uses PM tools, growing, tech/consulting/agency. Output: score 1-10, summary, next step, draft outreach for top contact.
02-lead-research-and-qualification.successMessage: 30 minutes of research compressed into 30 seconds. AI-qualified leads let you focus on the ones worth calling.

02-lead-research-and-qualification.instruction: Analyze this lead qualification criteria and identify what's missing: 'We qualify leads based on company size and whether they responded to our email.'
02-lead-research-and-qualification.hint: Missing: budget/authority indicators, timeline urgency, technology fit, pain point alignment, industry fit, engagement scoring beyond email response, competitor usage, growth signals, and decision-making process complexity.
02-lead-research-and-qualification.goodExample: Missing critical dimensions: 1) Budget — no spend threshold. 2) Authority — no decision-maker check. 3) Need — no pain/solution alignment. 4) Timeline — no urgency signals. 5) Tech fit — no stack check. 6) Behavioral signals — only email response, ignoring website visits, content engagement. This is a 2-factor model pretending to be qualification.
02-lead-research-and-qualification.successMessage: Qualification without BANT is just guessing. The more dimensions you score, the better your pipeline quality.

03-outreach-that-converts.instruction: Write a prompt to generate a personalized cold outreach email. Include specific prospect context, your value proposition, a hook, and a soft CTA.
03-outreach-that-converts.placeholder: Write a personalized cold email to...
03-outreach-that-converts.hint: Write a cold outreach email to Sarah Chen, VP of Marketing at GrowthCo (Series B, 200 employees, just hired 5 content writers). I sell AI content tools ($200/mo). Hook: reference their hiring spree. Value: each writer produces 3x more content. CTA: 'Would a 15-min demo be worth your time?' Under 150 words, warm professional tone.
03-outreach-that-converts.badExample: Write a cold email to a marketing VP about our product
03-outreach-that-converts.goodExample: Cold email to Sarah Chen, VP Marketing at GrowthCo (Series B, 200 employees, just hired 5 content writers). I sell AI content tools ($200/mo). Hook: their content team expansion. Value: each writer produces 3x more, similar company saved 40%. CTA: 'Worth 15 min this week?' Under 150 words, warm professional tone.
03-outreach-that-converts.successMessage: Personalization at scale is the AI sales superpower. Generic outreach is dead — context-rich outreach converts.

03-outreach-that-converts.instruction: Rewrite this generic cold email into a personalized, compelling outreach: 'Hi, I noticed your company might benefit from our solution. We help businesses grow. Would you like to schedule a call?'
03-outreach-that-converts.placeholder: Transform this generic email into something specific...
03-outreach-that-converts.hint: Hi Sarah, I saw GrowthCo just closed Series B and is scaling the content team — congrats! When we helped a similar 200-person SaaS company scale content, their writers went from 3 to 10 articles/week each. With 5 new writers ramping up, that could save your team months. Worth a 15-min walkthrough this week?
03-outreach-that-converts.badExample: Hi, I noticed your company might benefit from our solution. We help businesses grow. Would you like to schedule a call?
03-outreach-that-converts.goodExample: Hi Sarah, saw GrowthCo just closed Series B — congrats! When a similar SaaS company scaled their content team, our AI tools helped writers go from 3 to 10 articles/week each. With 5 new writers ramping up, that could save months. Worth a 15-min walkthrough this week?
03-outreach-that-converts.successMessage: Feel the difference? Specific beats generic every time. The prospect should feel like you wrote this just for them.

04-sales-calls-and-meetings.instruction: Write a prompt to generate a pre-call research brief for an upcoming sales meeting. Include company research, stakeholders, likely objections, and conversation starters.
04-sales-calls-and-meetings.placeholder: Prepare a pre-call brief for my meeting with...
04-sales-calls-and-meetings.hint: Pre-call brief for DataFlow Inc meeting (Series A, 80 employees, analytics platform). Attendees: CTO Maria Rodriguez, Head of Eng James Park. Selling API monitoring ($300/mo). Research their product launches, tech blog, job listings. Predict top 3 objections. 3 conversation starters from recent news. Discovery question per stakeholder by role.
04-sales-calls-and-meetings.badExample: Prepare me for a sales call tomorrow
04-sales-calls-and-meetings.goodExample: Pre-call brief for DataFlow Inc (Series A, 80 employees, analytics platform). Attendees: CTO Maria Rodriguez, Head of Eng James Park. Selling API monitoring ($300/mo). Research: product launches, tech blog, job listings. Predict top 3 objections. 3 conversation starters from news. Discovery question per attendee by role.
04-sales-calls-and-meetings.successMessage: Walking in prepared is the difference between closing and losing. AI makes preparation effortless.

04-sales-calls-and-meetings.instruction: Write a prompt to summarize a sales call transcript and extract action items, objections raised, buying signals, and recommended next steps.
04-sales-calls-and-meetings.placeholder: Summarize this sales call and extract...
04-sales-calls-and-meetings.hint: Analyze this 30-minute sales call transcript. Extract: key discussion points (bullets), objections (with quotes), buying signals, commitments made, action items with owners/deadlines, deal stage recommendation, risk factors. Format as CRM-ready brief.
04-sales-calls-and-meetings.goodExample: Analyze this 30-min sales call transcript. Extract: key points (bullets), objections (with quotes), buying signals, commitments, action items with owners/deadlines, deal stage recommendation, risk factors. Format as a CRM-ready brief.
04-sales-calls-and-meetings.successMessage: Post-call summaries in seconds, not 20 minutes. Every detail captured, nothing slipping through the cracks.

05-proposal-and-pitch-creation.instruction: Write a prompt to generate a tailored sales proposal outline. Include the client's pain points, your solution mapping, pricing approach, and ROI projection.
05-proposal-and-pitch-creation.hint: Proposal for DataFlow Inc. Pain: manual API monitoring (3 engineers, 10 hrs/week), slow outage detection (45 min avg), no capacity planning data. Solution: our tool ($300/mo), detection in <2 min, analytics dashboard. Include: exec summary, problem-to-solution mapping, 2-week implementation, annual pricing, ROI calc, 3 testimonials from similar companies.
05-proposal-and-pitch-creation.badExample: Write a proposal for a client about our monitoring tool
05-proposal-and-pitch-creation.goodExample: Proposal for DataFlow Inc. Pain: manual API monitoring (3 engineers, 10 hrs/week), slow detection (45 min avg), no capacity data. Solution: our tool ($300/mo), <2 min detection, analytics dashboard. Include: exec summary, problem→solution mapping, 2-week plan, annual pricing option, ROI calc, 3 similar-company testimonials.
05-proposal-and-pitch-creation.successMessage: Custom proposals win deals. AI makes the customization effortless while keeping quality sky-high.

05-proposal-and-pitch-creation.instruction: Rewrite this weak pitch into a compelling elevator pitch: 'We have a tool that monitors APIs. It's really good and lots of companies use it. You should try it.'
05-proposal-and-pitch-creation.placeholder: Transform this weak pitch into something compelling...
05-proposal-and-pitch-creation.hint: What if you could detect API outages in under 2 minutes instead of 45? Our monitoring platform saves engineering teams 10+ hours/week and has prevented $2M in downtime costs for companies your size. Your competitors are already using it.
05-proposal-and-pitch-creation.badExample: We have a tool that monitors APIs. It's really good and lots of companies use it. You should try it.
05-proposal-and-pitch-creation.goodExample: What if you detected API outages in 2 minutes, not 45? Our monitoring platform saves engineering teams 10+ hours/week and has prevented $2M+ in downtime costs. Your competitors are already using it — can you afford not to?
05-proposal-and-pitch-creation.successMessage: Specifics sell. Vague pitches die. Numbers, results, and urgency turn 'interesting' into 'where do I sign?'

06-objection-handling.instruction: Write a prompt to generate responses to the 5 most common sales objections in your industry. Each response should: acknowledge, reframe, and provide evidence.
06-objection-handling.hint: Generate responses to 5 common B2B SaaS objections ($5K/yr deals): 'too expensive', 'built internal tool', 'bad timing', 'need boss approval', 'locked into competitor'. Each: acknowledge (validate), reframe (shift perspective), evidence (case study/stat), bridge (back to value). Consultative tone.
06-objection-handling.badExample: How do I handle sales objections?
06-objection-handling.goodExample: Generate responses to 5 common B2B SaaS objections ($5K/yr deals): 'too expensive', 'built internal tool', 'bad timing', 'need boss approval', 'locked into competitor'. Each response: acknowledge (validate), reframe (shift perspective), evidence (case study/stat), bridge (back to value). Consultative tone, not pushy.
06-objection-handling.successMessage: Polished objection responses ready to deploy. Never freeze in a meeting again.

06-objection-handling.instruction: Analyze this objection response and identify what's wrong: Prospect says 'It's too expensive.' Rep responds: 'Actually, we're cheaper than most competitors. Do you want to see a comparison?'
06-objection-handling.hint: Problems: 1) 'Actually' is confrontational. 2) Jumps to price comparison without understanding budget concern. 3) Doesn't acknowledge the concern. 4) Competing on price instead of reframing to value/ROI. 5) No evidence or social proof.
06-objection-handling.badExample: The response seems fine to me
06-objection-handling.goodExample: Fails on 4 levels: 1) 'Actually' is confrontational — invalidates the prospect. 2) No acknowledgment of concern. 3) Competing on price instead of reframing to value/ROI. 4) No evidence or social proof. Better: validate → ask what they're comparing to → share ROI case study → offer personalized ROI calc.
06-objection-handling.successMessage: The best objection handlers don't fight the objection — they redirect to value. That's a learnable skill.

07-pipeline-management.instruction: Design an AI-powered pipeline review system: describe how AI would analyze your deals and flag which need attention, which are at risk, and which are ready to close.
07-pipeline-management.placeholder: The AI pipeline system would analyze...
07-pipeline-management.hint: AI Pipeline Review: Inputs — CRM fields, email engagement, call transcripts, meeting frequency. Risk signals — no contact 14+ days, champion silent, competitor mentioned, stuck stage 30+ days. Close signals — budget confirmed, timeline set, multiple stakeholders engaged. Output: weekly ranked digest, daily top-3 actions, real-time alerts when health drops below 50.
07-pipeline-management.badExample: AI would look at the deals and tell me which ones are good
07-pipeline-management.goodExample: System ingests: CRM data, email opens, call transcripts, meeting cadence. Risk flags: 14+ days no contact, champion silent, competitor mentioned, stuck 30+ days. Close signals: budget confirmed, timeline set, proposal opened. Output: weekly ranked digest, daily top-3 actions, real-time alerts on health score drops.
07-pipeline-management.successMessage: AI catches the deals you'd miss in a manual pipeline review. This is sales management at scale.

07-pipeline-management.instruction: Write a prompt to analyze a stalled deal and recommend actions to restart it. Include the deal context and what data AI should examine.
07-pipeline-management.placeholder: Analyze this stalled deal and recommend...
07-pipeline-management.hint: Stalled deal: Acme Corp, $25K/yr, stuck in Evaluation 6 weeks (normal: 2). Last contact: email 18 days ago, no reply. Champion went quiet after internal reorg. Analyze: email history, org changes, competitor activity, original pain points. Recommend: 3 re-engagement strategies, new outreach draft, go-around assessment, honest kill/continue recommendation.
07-pipeline-management.goodExample: Stalled deal: Acme Corp, $25K/yr, stuck in Evaluation 6 weeks (normal: 2). Last contact: email 18 days ago, no reply. Champion quiet after reorg. Analyze: email history, org changes, competitor activity, original pain points. Recommend: 3 ranked re-engagement strategies, new outreach draft, go-around assessment, kill/continue call.
07-pipeline-management.successMessage: Stalled deals aren't dead — they're misdiagnosed. AI helps you find the real blocker and act on it.

08-customer-success-with-ai.instruction: Write a prompt to analyze customer usage data, generate a health score, and recommend retention actions.
08-customer-success-with-ai.placeholder: Analyze this customer's usage and generate...
08-customer-success-with-ai.hint: Customer health for TechStart Inc ($500/mo, 12 months). Data: logins 45→12/week over 3 months, 2/5 seats active, new feature unused, support tickets 3x, NPS 8→5. Generate: health score (1-100), churn risk, root cause hypothesis, 3 ranked retention actions, draft check-in email.
08-customer-success-with-ai.badExample: Is this customer going to churn?
08-customer-success-with-ai.goodExample: Customer health analysis for TechStart Inc ($500/mo, 12 months). Data: logins 45→12/week over 3 months, 2/5 seats active, new feature unused, support tickets 3x, NPS 8→5. Generate: health score (1-100) by category, churn risk level, root cause hypothesis, 3 ranked retention actions, draft check-in email.
08-customer-success-with-ai.successMessage: Proactive customer success powered by AI prevents churn before the cancellation email lands.

08-customer-success-with-ai.instruction: Design an AI-powered early warning system for customer churn. List the signals it monitors, thresholds that trigger alerts, and escalation steps.
08-customer-success-with-ai.hint: Signals: login frequency (40% drop = alert), feature adoption (<30% at 60 days), support sentiment (3+ negative tickets), NPS trend (2+ point drop), billing failures, champion turnover. Escalation: Yellow (1 signal) → CSM alert. Orange (2) → manager + exec check-in. Red (3+) → VP involved + retention offer.
08-customer-success-with-ai.badExample: Watch for customers who stop logging in
08-customer-success-with-ai.goodExample: Monitors: login frequency (40% drop = alert), feature adoption (<30% at 60 days), support sentiment (3+ negative tickets), NPS (2+ point drop), billing failures, champion turnover. Escalation: Yellow (1 signal) → CSM Slack alert. Orange (2) → manager + exec check-in. Red (3+) → VP involved + retention offer ready.
08-customer-success-with-ai.successMessage: A churn early warning system turns reactive firefighting into proactive retention.

09-sales-analytics.instruction: Write a prompt to analyze a quarter of sales data and surface actionable insights. Specify what data is available, questions to answer, and desired output format.
09-sales-analytics.hint: Analyze Q1 2026 sales: 150 deals, sizes $1K-$50K, with cycle length, lead source, rep performance, industry, product line. Answer: win rate by source, cycle time by size, top 3 loss reasons, rep performance spread, industry trends. Output: 5-bullet exec summary, detailed analysis, 3 Q2 recommendations.
09-sales-analytics.goodExample: Analyze Q1 2026 sales: 150 deals, sizes $1K-$50K, with cycle length, lead source, rep, industry, product line. Answer: win rate by source, cycle time by deal size, top 3 loss reasons, rep performance spread, industry trends. Output: 5-bullet exec summary, detailed analysis, 3 Q2 recommendations.
09-sales-analytics.successMessage: Data-driven sales strategy beats gut instinct every time. These insights reshape your next quarter.

09-sales-analytics.instruction: Write a prompt to create a sales forecasting model with historical data, variables to consider, and accuracy metrics.
09-sales-analytics.hint: Q2 forecast from: 4 quarters historical, 80 active deals ($2.1M pipeline), 8 reps (avg 5 deals/mo), 15% YoY market growth. Variables: source conversion, deal size trends, seasonality, stage probabilities. Output: 3 scenarios with monthly breakdown, assumptions, confidence levels, top 3 risks.
09-sales-analytics.goodExample: Q2 forecast from: 4 quarters historical data, 80 active deals ($2.1M pipeline), 8 reps (avg 5 deals/mo), 15% YoY market growth. Variables: source conversion, deal size trends, seasonality, stage probabilities. Output: conservative/likely/optimistic scenarios with monthly breakdown, assumptions, confidence levels, top 3 risk factors.
09-sales-analytics.successMessage: A forecast with scenarios and assumptions is a strategic tool. A single number is a guess.

10-your-ai-sales-system.instruction: Design your complete AI sales system: map each stage (prospecting, outreach, meetings, proposals, closing, post-sale) and describe what AI handles at each.
10-your-ai-sales-system.hint: Prospecting: AI scrapes + qualifies leads, scores ICP fit. Outreach: personalizes emails at scale, A/B tests, optimizes timing. Meetings: pre-call briefs, transcription, action extraction. Proposals: custom drafts from templates + call notes. Closing: close probability, negotiation tactics. Post-sale: health monitoring, churn flags, upsell timing.
10-your-ai-sales-system.goodExample: Prospecting: AI scrapes leads, scores ICP fit, enriches data. Outreach: personalizes emails, A/B tests, optimizes timing. Meetings: pre-call briefs, transcription, action extraction. Proposals: custom drafts from templates + notes, per-prospect ROI. Closing: probability scoring, negotiation recs. Post-sale: health monitoring, churn flags, upsell timing. Human stays in: relationships, final negotiations, strategy.
10-your-ai-sales-system.successMessage: End-to-end AI sales system designed. This is how top performers scale without losing the human touch.

10-your-ai-sales-system.instruction: Create a 30-day implementation plan for rolling out AI across your sales process. Week by week: what tools, what training, what metrics to track.
10-your-ai-sales-system.hint: Week 1: AI prospect research. Tool: Claude. Train: research prompts. Metric: time/lead. Week 2: AI outreach emails. Tool: Claude + templates. Train: personalization prompts. Metric: reply rate. Week 3: Call prep + summaries. Tool: Claude + transcription. Train: brief workflow. Metric: prep time saved. Week 4: Pipeline analytics. Tool: Claude + CRM data. Train: analysis prompts. Metric: forecast accuracy.
10-your-ai-sales-system.badExample: Just start using AI in sales
10-your-ai-sales-system.goodExample: Week 1: AI prospect research. Tool: Claude. Training: research prompts. Metric: time/lead. Week 2: AI outreach. Tool: Claude + templates. Training: personalization. Metric: reply rate. Week 3: Call prep + summaries. Tool: Claude + transcription. Training: brief workflow. Metric: prep time. Week 4: Pipeline analytics. Tool: Claude + CRM. Training: analysis. Metric: forecast accuracy.
10-your-ai-sales-system.successMessage: A phased rollout builds momentum. By week 4, your team will wonder how they sold without AI.
